<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `pigpio` library to read temperature and humidity data from a DHT11 sensor connected to GPIO 7, displaying readings to console every 5 seconds with timestamps, implementing robust error handling for sensor communication failures and invalid data readings. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>

#define DHT11_GPIO 7

// Structure to hold temperature and humidity data
typedef struct {
    float temperature;
    float humidity;
    int error;
} DHT11Data;

// Global variable to indicate program termination
volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

// Signal handler for Ctrl+C
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    program_running = 0;
    printf("\nExiting gracefully...\n");
}

// Function to read data from the DHT11 sensor
DHT11Data read_dht11() {
    int data[5] = {0, 0, 0, 0, 0};
    int bit_count = 0;
    int pulse_count = 0;
    long start_time, end_time;

    gpioSetMode(DHT11_GPIO, PI_OUTPUT); // Set GPIO as output
    gpioWrite(DHT11_GPIO, 0);          // Send start signal
    gpioDelay(1);                       // Wait for 1ms
    gpioWrite(DHT11_GPIO, 1);
    gpioDelay(30);
    gpioSetMode(DHT11_GPIO, PI_INPUT); // Set GPIO as input

    // Read 40 bits of data
    for (int i = 0; i < 40; i++) {
        // Wait for low pulse
        while (gpioRead(DHT11_GPIO) == 1) {
            pulse_count++;
            if (pulse_count > 100) {
                DHT11Data error_data = {0.0, 0.0, 1}; // Indicate error
                return error_data;
            }
            gpioDelay(1);
        }

        pulse_count = 0;

        // Wait for high pulse
        while (gpioRead(DHT11_GPIO) == 0) {
            pulse_count++;
            if (pulse_count > 100) {
                DHT11Data error_data = {0.0, 0.0, 1}; // Indicate error
                return error_data;
            }
            gpioDelay(1);
        }

        // Measure the length of the high pulse
        start_time = gpioTick();
        while (gpioRead(DHT11_GPIO) == 1) {
            end_time = gpioTick();
            if (end_time - start_time > 100) {
                DHT11Data error_data = {0.0, 0.0, 1}; // Indicate error
                return error_data;
            }
        }

        // Determine if the bit is a 0 or a 1 based on the pulse length
        if (end_time - start_time > 40) {
            data[i / 8] |= (1 << (7 - (i % 8)));
        }
    }

    // Validate checksum
    if ((data[0] + data[1] + data[2] + data[3]) != data[4]) {
        DHT11Data error_data = {0.0, 0.0, 1}; // Indicate error
        return error_data;
    }

    DHT11Data result;
    result.temperature = (float)((data[2] & 0x7F) * 256 + data[3]) / 10.0;
    if (data[2] & 0x80) {
        result.temperature = -result.temperature;
    }
    result.humidity = (float)(data[0] * 256 + data[1]) / 10.0;

    return result;
}

// Function to display temperature and humidity data
void display_data(float temperature, float humidity) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm * timeinfo;
    char timestamp[80];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    printf("[%s] Temperature: %.1f\u00b0C, Humidity: %.1f%%\n", timestamp, temperature, humidity);
}

int main() {
    // Initialize pigpio library
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Main loop
    while (program_running) {
        DHT11Data data = read_dht11();

        if (data.error == 0) {
            display_data(data.temperature, data.humidity);
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error reading DHT11 sensor\n");
        }

        gpioDelay(5000); // Wait for 5 seconds
    }

    // Terminate pigpio library
    gpioTerminate();

    return 0;
}

        return data; // Return invalid data
    }

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <wiringPi.h>

// Define the GPIO pin for the buzzer (WiringPi pin number)
#define BUZZER_PIN 1

// Function prototypes
void beep(int duration_ms);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Global variable to track the program's running state
volatile sig_atomic_t running = 1;

// Signal handler function to catch Ctrl+C and terminate the program gracefully
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    if (signal == SIGINT) {
        printf("\nCaught SIGINT, exiting gracefully...\n");
        running = 0; // Set the running flag to 0 to exit the main loop
    }
}

// Function to beep the buzzer for a specified duration
void beep(int duration_ms) {
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, HIGH); // Turn the buzzer ON
    delay(duration_ms);             // Wait for the specified duration
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);  // Turn the buzzer OFF
    delay(duration_ms);             // Wait for the specified duration
}

int main(void) {
    // Initialize wiringPi
    if (wiringPiSetup() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error initializing wiringPi!\n");
        return 1; // Exit with an error code
    }

    // Set the buzzer pin as an output
    pinMode(BUZZER_PIN, OUTPUT);

    // Register the signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    printf("Buzzer control program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop to beep the buzzer continuously
    while (running) {
        beep(2000); // Beep for 2 seconds
        delay(1000); // Wait for 1 second

        time_t current_time;
        time(&current_time);
        char* time_string = ctime(&current_time);
        time_string[strlen(time_string) - 1] = '\0'; // Remove newline character

        printf("[%s] Buzzer ON\n", time_string);

        beep(2000); // Beep for 2 seconds
        delay(1000); // Wait for 1 second

        time(&current_time);
        time_string = ctime(&current_time);
        time_string[strlen(time_string) - 1] = '\0'; // Remove newline character

        printf("[%s] Buzzer OFF\n", time_string);
    }

    // Clean up and exit
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW); // Ensure buzzer is off
    pinMode(BUZZER_PIN, INPUT);    // Set pin to input to avoid spurious output
    printf("Program finished.\n");
    return 0;
}

    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    while (program_running) {

        digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, HIGH);
        time(&rawtime);
        timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
        strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);
        printf("%s Buzzer ON\n", timestamp);
        delay(2000);

        // Turn the buzzer off
        digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);
        time(&rawtime);
        timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
        strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);
        printf("%s Buzzer OFF\n", timestamp);
        delay(3000);
    }

    // Clean up GPIO before exiting
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW); // Ensure buzzer is off
    return 0;
}

#include <stdlib.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <wiringPi.h>

#define BUZZER_PIN 18

volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

void signal_handler(int signal) {
    program_running = 0;
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, exiting gracefully.\n", signal);
}

int main(void) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm * timeinfo;
    char timestamp[80];

    // Initialize wiringPi using GPIO numbering
    if (wiringPiSetupGpio() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error initializing wiringPi.\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set the buzzer pin as output
    pinMode(BUZZER_PIN, OUTPUT);

    // Register the signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    printf("Buzzer control program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (program_running) {
        // Turn the buzzer on
        digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, HIGH);
        time(&rawtime);
        timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
        strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);
        printf("%s Buzzer ON\n", timestamp);
        delay(2000);

        // Turn the buzzer off
        digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);
        time(&rawtime);
        timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
        strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);
        printf("%s Buzzer OFF\n", timestamp);
        delay(3000);
    }

    // Clean up GPIO before exiting
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW); // Ensure buzzer is off
    return 0;
}

input: Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `wiringPi` library to control an active buzzer connected to GPIO 18, turning it on for 2 seconds and off for 3 seconds in a continuous loop, displaying the current state to console with timestamps, handling GPIO initialization errors gracefully.
inference_response:
<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `wiringPi` library to control an active buzzer connected to GPIO 18, turning it on for 2 seconds and off for 3 seconds in a continuous loop, displaying the current state to console with timestamps, handling GPIO initialization errors gracefully. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <unistd.h>

#define BUZZER_PIN 18

// Global variable to track program termination
volatile sig_atomic_t terminate = 0;

// Signal handler function
void signal_handler(int sig)
{
    fprintf(stderr, "\nCaught signal %d, terminating gracefully...\n", sig);
    terminate = 1;
}

// Function to get current timestamp as a string
void get_timestamp(char *timestamp)
{
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, 20, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);
}

// Function to control the buzzer
void control_buzzer(int state)
{
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, state);
}

int main()
{
    // Register signal handlers for SIGINT and SIGTERM
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    // Initialize pigpio library
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0)
    {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO 18 as an output
    gpioSetMode(BUZZER_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Main loop
    while (!terminate)
    {
        // Turn the buzzer on
        control_buzzer(1);
        char timestamp[20];
        get_timestamp(timestamp);
        printf("%s Buzzer ON\n", timestamp);

        // Wait for 2 seconds
        sleep(2);

        // Turn the buzzer off
        control_buzzer(0);
        get_timestamp(timestamp);
        printf("%s Buzzer OFF\n", timestamp);

        // Wait for 3 seconds
        sleep(3);
    }

    // Clean up and terminate
    control_buzzer(0); // Ensure buzzer is off
    gpioTerminate();
    fprintf(stderr, "Program terminated.\n");
    return 0;
}

task: Create a Periodic Active Buzzer Control Simple_Actuator Application for Testing Purpose
input: Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `wiringPi` library to control an active buzzer connected to GPIO 18, turning it on for 2 seconds and off for 3 seconds in a continuous loop, displaying the current state to console with timestamps, handling GPIO initialization errors gracefully.
inference_response:
<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using the `wiringPi` library to control an active buzzer connected to GPIO 18, turning it on for 2 seconds and off for 3 seconds in a continuous loop, displaying the current state to console with timestamps, handling GPIO initialization errors gracefully. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <wiringPi.h>

#define BUZZER_PIN 18 // GPIO 18 (WiringPi pin number)

volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, exiting gracefully.\n", signal);
    program_running = 0;
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW); // Ensure buzzer is off
    exit(0);
}

// Function to display the buzzer state with timestamp
void display_state(int state) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
    char timestamp[20];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    if (state == HIGH) {
        printf("[%s] Buzzer ON\n", timestamp);
    } else {
        printf("[%s] Buzzer OFF\n", timestamp);
    }
}

int main(void) {
    // Set up signal handling for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize WiringPi using GPIO numbering
    if (wiringPiSetupGpio() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error initializing WiringPi. Please check your setup and permissions.\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set the buzzer pin as output
    pinMode(BUZZER_PIN, OUTPUT);

    printf("Buzzer control program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (program_running) {
        // Turn the buzzer on
        digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, HIGH);
        display_state(HIGH);
        delay(2000); // 2 seconds

        // Turn the buzzer off
        digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);
        display_state(LOW);
        delay(3000); // 3 seconds
    }

    return 0;
}

input: Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to control three LEDs connected to GPIO pins 16, 20, and 21, creating a sequential blinking pattern where each LED lights up for 1 second in sequence, printing the active LED number to console every second, with robust error handling for `pigpio` initialization.

<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to control three LEDs connected to GPIO pins 16, 20, and 21, creating a sequential blinking pattern where each LED lights up for 1 second in sequence, printing the active LED number to console every second, with robust error handling for `pigpio` initialization. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>

// Define the GPIO pins for the LEDs
#define LED_PIN_1 16
#define LED_PIN_2 20
#define LED_PIN_3 21

// Function to initialize the GPIO pins
int initialize_gpio() {
    // Initialize the pigpio library
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    // Set the GPIO pins as outputs
    if (gpioSetMode(LED_PIN_1, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED_PIN_1);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED_PIN_2, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED_PIN_2);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED_PIN_3, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED_PIN_3);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Function to turn an LED on
void led_on(int gpio_pin) {
    gpioWrite(gpio_pin, 1); // Set the GPIO pin to HIGH
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm * timeinfo;
    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
    char timestamp[20];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);
    printf("%s: LED on (GPIO %d)\n", timestamp, gpio_pin);
}

// Function to turn an LED off
void led_off(int gpio_pin) {
    gpioWrite(gpio_pin, 0); // Set the GPIO pin to LOW
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm * timeinfo;
    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
    char timestamp[20];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);
    printf("%s: LED off (GPIO %d)\n", timestamp, gpio_pin);
}

// Function for the blinking sequence
void blink_sequence() {
    // LED 1 on
    led_on(LED_PIN_1);
    gpioDelay(1000); // Wait for 1 second
    led_off(LED_PIN_1);

    // LED 2 on
    led_on(LED_PIN_2);
    gpioDelay(1000); // Wait for 1 second
    led_off(LED_PIN_2);

    // LED 3 on
    led_on(LED_PIN_3);
    gpioDelay(1000); // Wait for 1 second
    led_off(LED_PIN_3);
}

int main() {
    // Initialize the GPIO pins
    if (initialize_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1; // Exit if initialization fails
    }

    // Main loop for the blinking sequence
    while (1) {
        blink_sequence();
    }

    // Terminate the pigpio library (this part will not be reached in the infinite loop)
    gpioTerminate();

    return 0;
}

/*
To compile:
gcc -o led_blink led_blink.c -lpigpio

To run:
sudo ./led_blink

You need to have the pigpio daemon running before executing the program:
sudo pigpiod
*/

    }

    return 0;

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    if (signal == SIGINT) {

        program_running = 0;
    }
}

#include <signal.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins for LEDs
#define LED1_PIN 16
#define LED2_PIN 20
#define LED3_PIN 21

// Global variable to indicate program termination
volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

// Signal handler for Ctrl+C
void signal_handler(int signum) {
    program_running = 0;
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, exiting gracefully...\n", signum);
}

// Function to initialize GPIO pins
int initialize_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    if (gpioSetMode(LED1_PIN, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED1_PIN);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED2_PIN, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED2_PIN);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED3_PIN, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED3_PIN);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Function to blink LEDs in sequence
void blink_leds() {
    int leds[] = {LED1_PIN, LED2_PIN, LED3_PIN};
    int num_leds = sizeof(leds) / sizeof(leds[0]);

    for (int i = 0; i < num_leds && program_running; ++i) {
        // Turn on the LED
        if (gpioWrite(leds[i], 1) != 0) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Failed to write to GPIO %d\n", leds[i]);
            continue; // Continue to the next LED
        }
        printf("LED %d turned ON\n", leds[i]);
        sleep(1);

        // Turn off the LED
        if (gpioWrite(leds[i], 0) != 0) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Failed to write to GPIO %d\n", leds[i]);
            continue; // Continue to the next LED
        }
        printf("LED %d turned OFF\n", leds[i]);
        sleep(1);
    }
}

// Function to cleanup GPIO
void cleanup_gpio() {
    gpioTerminate();
    printf("pigpio terminated\n");
}

int main() {
    // Register signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (initialize_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1; // Exit if initialization fails
    }

    printf("LED blinking started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Blink LEDs until Ctrl+C is pressed
    while (program_running) {
        blink_leds();
    }

    // Cleanup GPIO before exiting
    cleanup_gpio();

    return 0;
}

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define the GPIO pins connected to the LEDs
#define LED_1 16
#define LED_2 20
#define LED_3 21

// Global variable to control the main loop
volatile sig_atomic_t running = 1;

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signum) {
    running = 0;
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, stopping...\n", signum);
}

int main() {
    int gpio_status[3];
    int i;

    // Initialize the pigpio library
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set the GPIO pins as outputs
    if (gpioSetMode(LED_1, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED_1);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED_2, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED_2);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED_3, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED_3);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Register the signal handler for SIGINT (Ctrl+C)
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Main loop
    while (running) {
        // Get the status of each LED
        gpio_status[0] = gpioRead(LED_1);
        gpio_status[1] = gpioRead(LED_2);
        gpio_status[2] = gpioRead(LED_3);

        // Print the status of each LED
        printf("LED %d: %d, LED %d: %d, LED %d: %d\n", LED_1, gpio_status[0], LED_2, gpio_status[1], LED_3, gpio_status[2]);

        // Control the LEDs in sequence
        for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
            if (gpio_status[i] == 0) {
                gpioWrite(i + 16, 1); // Turn on the LED
                printf("LED %d ON\n", i + 16);
                gpioDelay(1000000); // Wait for 1 second
                gpioWrite(i + 16, 0); // Turn off the LED
                printf("LED %d OFF\n", i + 16);
            }
        }

        gpioDelay(100000); // Delay for 0.1 seconds
    }

    // Clean up and terminate the pigpio library
    gpioTerminate();
    printf("Program terminated gracefully.\n");
    return 0;
}

input: Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to control three LEDs connected to GPIO pins 16, 20, and 21, creating a sequential blinking pattern where each LED lights up for 1 second in sequence, printing the active LED number to console every second, with robust error handling for `pigpio` initialization.
inference_response:
<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to control three LEDs connected to GPIO pins 16, 20, and 21, creating a sequential blinking pattern where each LED lights up for 1 second in sequence, printing the active LED number to console every second, with robust error handling for `pigpio` initialization. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <unistd.h>

// Define the GPIO pins for the LEDs
#define LED1 16
#define LED2 20
#define LED3 21

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, cleaning up...\n", signal);
    // Turn off all LEDs
    gpioWrite(LED1, 0);
    gpioWrite(LED2, 0);
    gpioWrite(LED3, 0);
    // Terminate the pigpio library
    gpioTerminate();
    exit(0);
}

int main() {
    // Initialize the pigpio library
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set the GPIO pins as outputs
    if (gpioSetMode(LED1, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED1);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED2, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED2);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(LED3, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", LED3);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Register the signal handler for SIGINT (Ctrl+C)
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Main loop for the blinking sequence
    int led_number = 0;
    while (1) {
        // Turn on the LED
        gpioWrite(led_number, 1);
        printf("LED %d turned ON\n", led_number);
        sleep(1);

        // Turn off the LED
        gpioWrite(led_number, 0);
        printf("LED %d turned OFF\n", led_number);
        sleep(1);

        // Increment the LED number for the next iteration
        led_number = (led_number + 1) % 3;
    }

    return 0; // This line will never be reached due to the infinite loop
}

task: Create a IR Obstacle Avoidance Sensor Motion Detection File_Logging Application for Robot Navigation
input: Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` to continuously monitor an IR obstacle avoidance sensor with LM393 comparator connected to GPIO 22, logging obstacle detection events with timestamps and distance status to 'obstacle_log.txt', implementing debounce logic and handling sensor read errors with appropriate retry mechanisms.
inference_response:
<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` to continuously monitor an IR obstacle avoidance sensor with LM393 comparator connected to GPIO 22, logging obstacle detection events with timestamps and distance status to 'obstacle_log.txt', implementing debounce logic and handling sensor read errors with appropriate retry mechanisms. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <unistd.h>

#define SENSOR_GPIO 22
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY_US 5000
#define MAX_RETRIES 3

static volatile int running = 1;

void signal_handler(int sig) {
    running = 0;
    printf("Caught signal %d, exiting gracefully.\n", sig);
}

/*
 * Reads the sensor value with retries.
 */
int read_sensor_with_retry(int gpio) {
    int value = -1;
    int retries = 0;

    while (retries < MAX_RETRIES) {
        value = gpioRead(gpio);
        if (value >= 0) {
            return value;
        }
        gpioDelay(1000); // Wait 1ms before retrying
        retries++;
    }

    fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to read sensor value after %d retries.\n", MAX_RETRIES);
    return -1; // Indicate failure
}

/*
 * Debounces the sensor reading.
 */
int debounce_sensor(int gpio) {
    int value = read_sensor_with_retry(gpio);
    if (value < 0) {
        return -1; // Propagate read error
    }
    gpioDelay(DEBOUNCE_DELAY_US);
    return value;
}

/*
 * Logs the event to a file.
 */
void log_event(const char *timestamp, const char *status) {
    FILE *fp = fopen("obstacle_log.txt", "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Could not open obstacle_log.txt for writing.\n");
        return;
    }
    fprintf(fp, "%s - %s\n", timestamp, status);
    fclose(fp);
}

int main() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: pigpio initialisation failed.\n");
        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(SENSOR_GPIO, PI_INPUT);

    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    printf("Obstacle detection program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (running) {
        int sensor_value = debounce_sensor(SENSOR_GPIO);

        if (sensor_value < 0) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to read sensor value.\n");
            continue;
        }

        time_t now = time(NULL);
        struct tm *tstruct = localtime(&now);
        char timestamp[80];
        strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tstruct);

        if (sensor_value == 0) {
            log_event(timestamp, "Obstacle Detected");
            printf("%s - Obstacle Detected\n", timestamp);
        } else {
            log_event(timestamp, "No Obstacle");
            printf("%s - No Obstacle\n", timestamp);
        }

        gpioDelay(100000); // 100ms delay
    }

    gpioTerminate();
    printf("Program terminated.\n");
    return 0;
}

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

#define SENSOR_GPIO 22
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY_US 200000 // 200ms
#define LOG_FILE "obstacle_log.txt"

volatile sig_atomic_t terminate = 0;

void signal_handler(int sig) {
    terminate = 1;
    fprintf(stderr, "\nCaught signal %d, terminating gracefully...\n", sig);
}

// Function to read the sensor value with retry
int read_sensor() {
    int count = 0;
    int sensor_value;

    while (count < 3) {
        sensor_value = gpioRead(SENSOR_GPIO);
        if (sensor_value >= 0) {
            return sensor_value;
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error reading sensor (attempt %d): %d\n", count + 1, sensor_value);
            gpioDelay(1000); // Wait 1ms before retrying
            count++;
        }
    }

    return -1; // Return -1 if all retries failed
}

// Function to log the event to a file
void log_event(const char *status) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        perror("Error opening log file");
        return;
    }

    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm *tm = localtime(&t);
    char timestamp[64];

        return 1;

    // Install signal handlers
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    int last_state = 1; // Assume no obstacle initially (HIGH)

    printf("Obstacle detection started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (!terminate) {
        int sensor_value = read_sensor();

        if (sensor_value == -1) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Failed to read sensor after multiple retries. Continuing...\n");
            gpioDelay(100000); // Wait 100ms before next attempt
            continue;
        }

        if (sensor_value == 0 && last_state == 1) {
            // Obstacle detected
            log_event("Obstacle Detected");
            last_state = 0;
        } else if (sensor_value == 1 && last_state == 0) {
            // Obstacle cleared
            log_event("Obstacle Cleared");
            last_state = 1;
        }

        gpioDelay(DEBOUNCE_DELAY_US); // Debounce delay
    }

    gpioTerminate();
    printf("Obstacle detection stopped.\n");
    return 0;
}

#define GPIO_PIN 22

#define MAX_RETRIES 3

static volatile int running = 1;

void signal_handler(int signal) {
    running = 0;
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, exiting gracefully...\n", signal);
}

/*
 * Reads the sensor value with retry mechanism.
 */
int read_sensor() {
    int sensor_value = gpioRead(GPIO_PIN);
    if (sensor_value < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading sensor value: %d\n", sensor_value);
        return -1; // Indicate an error
    }
    return sensor_value;
}

/*
 * Logs the event to a file.

void log_event(const char *message) {
    FILE *fp = fopen("obstacle_log.txt", "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        perror("Error opening obstacle_log.txt");
        return;
    }

    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm *tm = localtime(&t);
    char timestamp[64];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tm);

    fprintf(fp, "%s: %s\n", timestamp, message);
    fclose(fp);
}

/*
 * Debounces the sensor input.
 */
bool debounce(int sensor_value) {
    gpioDelay(DEBOUNCE_DELAY_US);
    int next_value = gpioRead(GPIO_PIN);
    if (next_value < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading sensor value for debounce: %d\n", next_value);
        return false; // Return false on error
    }
    return (sensor_value == next_value);
}

    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(GPIO_PIN, PI_INPUT);

    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    int retries = 0;
    while (running) {
        int sensor_value = read_sensor();
        if (sensor_value >= 0) {
            if (debounce(sensor_value)) {
                if (sensor_value == 0) { // Obstacle detected (LOW signal)
                    log_event("Obstacle detected!");
                } else {
                    log_event("No obstacle detected.");
                }
            }
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "Failed to read sensor value. Retrying...\n");
            gpioDelay(100000); // Wait 100ms before retrying
            retries++;
            if (retries > MAX_RETRIES) {
                fprintf(stderr, "Max retries reached. Exiting.\n");
                break;
            }
        }
        gpioDelay(10000); // Small delay to avoid excessive CPU usage
    }

    gpioTerminate();
    return 0;
}

// Function to read the sensor value from the MCP3008
//	This uses the SPI bit banging method.

int readQ2W (int unit, int pin)
{
  int adcValue = -1 ;
  unsigned char buffer [3] ;

  buffer [0] = 0x01 ;	// Start bit
  buffer [1] = (0x08 | pin) << 4 ;
  buffer [2] = 0x00 ;

  spiXfer (unit, buffer, buffer, 3) ;

  adcValue = ((buffer [1] & 0x03) << 8) + buffer [2] ;

  return adcValue ;
}

 *	overloaded for other sensor types.

int readSensor (int unit, int pin)
{
  int value = readQ2W (unit, pin) ;

  if (value < 0)
    return -1 ;

  return value ;
}

{

void q2wSetLed (int led, int value)
{
  int pin ;

  if (led < 0)
    return ;

  pin = LED_BASE + led ;

  digitalWrite (pin, value) ;
}

#include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <string.h>

#define GPIO_PIN 22
#define LOG_FILE "obstacle_log.txt"
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY_MS 10
#define MAX_RETRIES 3

static volatile int running = 1;

// Function prototypes
int read_sensor(int gpio_pin);
void log_event(const char *status);
void signal_handler(int signal);

/*
 * Reads the sensor value with retry logic.
 */
int read_sensor(int gpio_pin) {
    int value = gpioRead(gpio_pin);
    if (value < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading sensor value: %d\n", value);
        return -1; // Indicate error
    }
    return value;
}

// Logs the event to a file.
void log_event(const char *status) {
    FILE *fp = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file: %s\n", LOG_FILE);
        return;
    }

    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm tm = *localtime(&t);
    char timestamp[20];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", &tm);

    fprintf(fp, "%s - %s\n", timestamp, status);
    fclose(fp);
}

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown.
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    running = 0;
    printf("Caught signal %d, exiting gracefully...\n", signal);
}

int main() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(GPIO_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetPullUpDown(GPIO_PIN, PI_PUD_UP);

    // Set up signal handling
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    int obstacle_detected = 0;
    int retries = 0;

    while (running) {
        int sensor_value = read_sensor(GPIO_PIN);

        if (sensor_value == -1) {
            // Handle sensor read error (e.g., log it)
            log_event("Error reading sensor");
            gpioDelay(100); // Wait before retrying
            retries++;
            if (retries > MAX_RETRIES) {
                fprintf(stderr, "Too many sensor read errors, exiting.\n");
                running = 0; // Exit loop after too many errors
            }
            continue; // Skip to the next iteration
        } else {
            retries = 0; // Reset retries on successful read
        }

        if (sensor_value == 0 && !obstacle_detected) {
            log_event("Obstacle detected");
            obstacle_detected = 1;
            gpioDelay(DEBOUNCE_DELAY_MS);
        } else if (sensor_value == 1 && obstacle_detected) {
            log_event("Obstacle cleared");
            obstacle_detected = 0;
        }

        gpioDelay(50); // Check every 50ms
    }

    gpioTerminate();
    return 0;
}

input: Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` to continuously monitor an IR obstacle avoidance sensor with LM393 comparator connected to GPIO 22, logging obstacle detection events with timestamps and distance status to 'obstacle_log.txt', implementing debounce logic and handling sensor read errors with appropriate retry mechanisms.

<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` to continuously monitor an IR obstacle avoidance sensor with LM393 comparator connected to GPIO 22, logging obstacle detection events with timestamps and distance status to 'obstacle_log.txt', implementing debounce logic and handling sensor read errors with appropriate retry mechanisms. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <string.h>

#define SENSOR_GPIO 22
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY_US 500
#define MAX_RETRIES 3

static volatile int terminate = 0;

// Structure to hold sensor data and state
typedef struct {
    int last_state;
    int retry_count;
} sensor_state_t;

void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("Caught signal %d, terminating gracefully...\n", signal);
    terminate = 1;
}

int read_sensor_with_retry(int gpio, int max_retries) {
    int reading = gpioRead(gpio);
    if (reading >= 0) {
        return reading;
    } else {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading GPIO %d: %d\n", gpio, reading);
        return -1; // Indicate error
    }
}

// Logs the event to the file
void log_event(const char *status) {
    FILE *fp = fopen("obstacle_log.txt", "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file!\n");
        return;
    }

    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm tm = *localtime(&t);
    fprintf(fp, "%d-%02d-%02d %02d:%02d:%02d - %s\n", tm.tm_year + 1900, tm.tm_mon + 1, tm.tm_mday, tm.tm_hour, tm.tm_min, tm.tm_sec, status);
    fclose(fp);
}

int main() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(SENSOR_GPIO, PI_INPUT);

    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    sensor_state_t sensor_state = {0, 0};

    while (!terminate) {
        int sensor_value = read_sensor_with_retry(SENSOR_GPIO, MAX_RETRIES);

        if (sensor_value >= 0) {
            if (sensor_value == 0 && sensor_state.last_state != 0) { // Obstacle detected
                log_event("Obstacle Detected");
                printf("Obstacle Detected\n");
            } else if (sensor_value == 1 && sensor_state.last_state != 1) { // No obstacle
                log_event("No Obstacle");
                printf("No Obstacle\n");
            }

            sensor_state.last_state = sensor_value;
            sensor_state.retry_count = 0;
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "Failed to read sensor value, retrying...\n");
            gpioDelay(DEBOUNCE_DELAY_US);
            sensor_state.retry_count++;
            if (sensor_state.retry_count >= MAX_RETRIES) {
                fprintf(stderr, "Max retries reached. Disabling sensor.\n");
                gpioSetMode(SENSOR_GPIO, PI_INPUT); // Set to input to avoid spurious output
                terminate = 1; // Exit loop
            }
        }

        gpioDelay(1000); // Check every 1 ms
    }

    gpioTerminate();
    printf("Program terminated.\n");
    return 0;
}

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>

#include <unistd.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define LED_PIN 18
#define SPI_CHANNEL 0
#define SPI_SPEED 1000000 // 1MHz

// Define light threshold (adjust as needed)
#define LIGHT_THRESHOLD 500

// Global variable for SPI file descriptor
int spi_fd;

// Function prototypes
int read_mcp3008(int channel);
void control_led(int light_level);
void print_status(int light_level, int led_state);
void cleanup(int signal);

// Signal handler for Ctrl+C
void cleanup(int signal) {
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, cleaning up...\n", signal);
    digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW); // Turn off LED
    if (spi_fd != -1) {
        close(spi_fd); // Close SPI channel
    }
    wiringPiSetupGpio(); // Reinitialize to avoid GPIO issues after cleanup
    digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW); // Ensure LED is off after reinitialization
    exit(0);
}

// Function to read data from MCP3008 ADC
int read_mcp3008(int channel) {
    unsigned char buffer[3];
    int adc_value = -1;

    buffer[0] = 0x01; // Start bit
    buffer[1] = ((channel & 0x07) << 4) | 0x80; // Single-ended mode, channel selection
    buffer[2] = 0x00;

    if (wiringPiSPIDataRW(SPI_CHANNEL, buffer, 3) == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "SPI communication failed!\n");
        return -1; // Indicate error
    }

    adc_value = ((buffer[1] & 0x03) << 8) + buffer[2];

    return adc_value;
}

// Function to control the LED based on light level
void control_led(int light_level) {
    if (light_level < LIGHT_THRESHOLD) {
        digitalWrite(LED_PIN, HIGH); // Turn on LED
    } else {
        digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW);  // Turn off LED
    }
}

        control_led(light_level);

// Define GPIO pins
#define SPI_CHANNEL 0
#define SPI_SPEED 1000000 // 1MHz
#define MCP3008_CE_PIN 8   // GPIO 8 (CE0)
#define LED_PIN 18

// Define light intensity threshold
#define LIGHT_THRESHOLD 500

// Define debounce delay (in milliseconds)
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY 200

// Global variables (to be minimized, but needed for signal handling)
volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

// Function prototypes
int read_mcp3008(int channel);
void control_led(int light_level);
void log_data(int light_level, int led_state);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Signal handler function
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    program_running = 0;
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, exiting gracefully.\n", signal);
}

// Function to read data from MCP3008 ADC
int read_mcp3008(int channel) {
    unsigned char buffer[3];
    int adc_value = -1;
    int retries = 3;

    buffer[0] = 0x01;                                      // Start bit
    buffer[1] = ((channel & 0x07) << 4) | 0x80;          // Single-ended mode, channel select
    buffer[2] = 0x00;                                      // Don't care

    while (retries > 0) {
        if (wiringPiSPIDataRW(SPI_CHANNEL, buffer, 3) == -1) {
            perror("SPI communication failed");
            retries--;
            delay(10); // Small delay before retrying
        } else {
            adc_value = ((buffer[1] & 0x03) << 8) + buffer[2];
            return adc_value;
        }
    }

    fprintf(stderr, "Failed to read MCP3008 after multiple retries.\n");
    return -1; // Indicate error
}

// Function to control the LED based on light intensity
void control_led(int light_level) {
    static int led_state = LOW; // Keep track of the current LED state
    static unsigned long last_change_time = 0; // Keep track of the last time the LED was changed
    unsigned long current_time = millis();

    if (light_level < LIGHT_THRESHOLD && led_state == LOW) {
        // Light level below threshold and LED is off
        if (current_time - last_change_time > DEBOUNCE_DELAY) {
            digitalWrite(LED_PIN, HIGH);
            led_state = HIGH;
            last_change_time = current_time;
        }
    } else if (light_level > LIGHT_THRESHOLD && led_state == HIGH) {
        // Light level above threshold and LED is on
        if (current_time - last_change_time > DEBOUNCE_DELAY) {
            digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW);
            led_state = LOW;
            last_change_time = current_time;
        }
    }
}

// Function to log data to the console
void log_data(int light_level, int led_state) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[80];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    printf("[%s] Light Level: %d, LED: %s\n", timestamp, light_level, led_state == HIGH ? "ON" : "OFF");
}

    // Initialize wiringPi
    if (wiringPiSetup() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "wiringPiSetup failed: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    // Initialize SPI
    if (wiringPiSPISetup(SPI_CHANNEL, SPI_SPEED) == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "wiringPiSPISetup failed: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    // Set LED pin as output
    pinMode(LED_PIN, OUTPUT);

    // Register signal handler for graceful shutdown
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    printf("Light-controlled LED program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (program_running) {
        // Read light intensity from MCP3008
        int light_level = read_mcp3008(0);

        if (light_level != -1) {
            // Control the LED
            control_led(light_level);

            // Log the data
            log_data(light_level, digitalRead(LED_PIN));
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "Failed to read light level.\n");
        }

        delay(2000); // Wait 2 seconds
    }

    // Clean up and exit
    digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW); // Turn off LED before exiting
    return 0;
}

#include <stdlib.h>
#include <stdint.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <wiringPi.h>
#include <wiringPiSPI.h>

// Define constants
#define SPI_CHANNEL 0
#define SPI_SPEED 1000000 // 1MHz
#define LDR_CHANNEL 0      // MCP3008 channel connected to LDR
#define LED_PIN 18         // GPIO pin connected to the LED
#define LIGHT_THRESHOLD 500 // Light intensity threshold

volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1; // Flag to control the main loop

// Function prototypes
int read_ldr_value();
void control_led(int light_level);
void print_status(int light_level, int led_state);
void cleanup(int signal);

// Signal handler for Ctrl+C
void cleanup(int signal) {
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, cleaning up and exiting...\n", signal);
    digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW); // Turn off the LED
    wiringPiSPIClose(SPI_CHANNEL); // Close SPI
    exit(0);
}

// Function to read LDR value from MCP3008
int read_ldr_value() {
    unsigned char buffer[3];
    int adc_value = 0;

    buffer[0] = 0x01;                           // Start bit
    buffer[1] = (0x08 | LDR_CHANNEL) << 4;      // Single-ended mode, channel selection
    buffer[2] = 0x00;                           // Don't care

    if (wiringPiSPIDataRW(SPI_CHANNEL, buffer, 3) == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "SPI communication error!\n");
        return -1; // Indicate error
    }

    adc_value = ((buffer[1] & 0x03) << 8) + buffer[2];

    return adc_value;
}

// Function to control the LED based on light level
void control_led(int light_level) {
    if (light_level < LIGHT_THRESHOLD) {
        digitalWrite(LED_PIN, HIGH); // Turn LED on
    } else {
        digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW);  // Turn LED off
    }
}

// Function to print light level and LED status
void print_status(int light_level, int led_state) {
    printf("Light Level: %d, LED: %s\n", light_level, led_state ? "ON" : "OFF");
}

int main() {
    // Setup signal handling
    signal(SIGINT, cleanup);

    // Initialize wiringPi
    if (wiringPiSetupGpio() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "wiringPiSetupGpio failed!\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Initialize SPI
    if (wiringPiSPISetup(SPI_CHANNEL, SPI_SPEED) == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "wiringPiSPISetup failed!\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set LED pin as output
    pinMode(LED_PIN, OUTPUT);

    printf("Light sensor and LED control program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (program_running) {
        int light_level = read_ldr_value();
        if (light_level != -1) {
            control_led(light_level);
            print_status(light_level, digitalRead(LED_PIN));
        } else {
            fprintf(stderr, "Failed to read light level, retrying...\n");
        }

        delay(2000); // Wait 2 seconds
    }

    return 0;
}

#define SPI_SPEED 1000000 // 1MHz

int read_ldr_value(int channel);
void control_led(int light_value, int threshold);
void cleanup_handler(int signo);

volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

int main(void) {
    int ldr_value, led_state;
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm * timeinfo;

    if (wiringPiSetupGpio() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "wiringPiSetupGpio failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Initialize SPI
    if (wiringPiSPISetup(SPI_CHANNEL, SPI_SPEED) == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "wiringPiSPISetup failed\n");
        return 1;

            fprintf(stderr, "Error reading LDR value\n");

            // Get current time
            time(&rawtime);
            timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

            printf("[%04d-%02d-%02d %02d:%02d:%02d] Light: %d, LED: %s\n",
                   timeinfo->tm_year + 1900, timeinfo->tm_mon + 1, timeinfo->tm_mday,
                   timeinfo->tm_hour, timeinfo->tm_min, timeinfo->tm_sec,
                   ldr_value, (led_state == 1) ? "ON" : "OFF");
        }

        // Wait 2 seconds
        delay(2000);
    }

    return 0;
}

// Function to read LDR value from MCP3008
int read_ldr_value(int channel) {
    unsigned char buffer[3];
    int adc_value = -1;

    // Construct SPI message
    buffer[0] = 0x01; // Start bit
    buffer[1] = (0x08 | channel) << 4; // Single-ended mode, channel selection
    buffer[2] = 0x00; // Don't care

    // Transmit and receive data
    if (wiringPiSPIDataRW(SPI_CHANNEL, buffer, 3) == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "SPI read/write failed\n");
        return -1;
    }

    // Parse ADC value
    adc_value = ((buffer[1] & 0x03) << 8) + buffer[2];

    return adc_value;
}

// Function to control LED based on light threshold
void control_led(int light_value, int threshold) {
    if (light_value < threshold) {
        digitalWrite(LED_PIN, HIGH); // Turn LED ON
    } else {
        digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW);  // Turn LED OFF
    }
}

void cleanup_handler(int signo) {
    program_running = 0;
    printf("\nExiting program...\n");
    digitalWrite(LED_PIN, LOW); // Turn off LED

<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to implement touch-activated relay control, where a TTP223 capacitive touch sensor on GPIO 25 triggers interrupts to toggle a 5V relay module connected to GPIO 26, logging each touch event and relay state change with timestamps to 'touch_relay.log', handling interrupt setup errors gracefully. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <string.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN 25
#define RELAY_PIN 26

// Debounce delay in milliseconds
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY 50

// Global variables
static volatile int relay_state = 0; // 0 = OFF, 1 = ON
static volatile int running = 1;
FILE *log_file = NULL;

// Function prototypes
void cleanup(int signum);
void log_event(const char *message);
void toggle_relay(void);
void touch_callback(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void cleanup(int signum) {
    running = 0;
    gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, 0); // Turn off relay
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }
    gpioTerminate();
    printf("\nExiting gracefully.\n");
    exit(0);
}

// Function to log events with timestamps
void log_event(const char *message) {
    time_t now = time(NULL);
    struct tm *tstruct = localtime(&now);
    char buf[80];
    strftime(buf, sizeof(buf), "%Y-%m-%d.%X", tstruct);

    if (log_file == NULL) {
        log_file = fopen("touch_relay.log", "a");
        if (log_file == NULL) {
            perror("Error opening log file");
            return;
        }
    }

    fprintf(log_file, "%s - %s\n", buf, message);
    fflush(log_file); // Ensure the log is written immediately
}

// Function to toggle the relay state
void toggle_relay(void) {
    relay_state = !relay_state;
    gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, relay_state);
    char message[100];
    sprintf(message, "Relay toggled to %s", relay_state ? "ON" : "OFF");
    log_event(message);
}

// Interrupt handler function
void touch_callback(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user) {
    static uint32_t last_tick = 0;
    uint32_t current_tick = gpioTick();

    // Debounce
    if (current_tick - last_tick < DEBOUNCE_DELAY * 1000) {
        return; // Ignore the touch event
    }
    last_tick = current_tick;

    toggle_relay();
}

int main(void) {
    // Initialize pigpio
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO modes
    gpioSetMode(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(RELAY_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to implement touch-activated relay control, where a TTP223 capacitive touch sensor on GPIO 25 triggers interrupts to toggle a 5V relay module connected to GPIO 26, logging each touch event and relay state change with timestamps to 'touch_relay.log', handling interrupt setup errors gracefully. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN 25
#define RELAY_PIN 26

// Debounce time in milliseconds
#define DEBOUNCE_TIME 50

// Global variables
static volatile int relay_state = 0; // 0 = OFF, 1 = ON
static volatile int running = 1;
static FILE *log_file = NULL;

// Function prototypes
int init_gpio();
void touch_callback(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data);
void toggle_relay(int current_state);
void log_event(int relay_state);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    running = 0;
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, exiting gracefully...\n", signal);
}

// Initialize GPIO pins
int init_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(RELAY_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Set pull-down resistor for the touch sensor pin
    gpioSetPullUpDown(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, PI_PUD_DOWN);

    return 0;
}

// Callback function for touch sensor interrupt
void touch_callback(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data) {
    // Debounce the touch sensor
    gpioDelay(DEBOUNCE_TIME * 1000); // Convert milliseconds to microseconds

    toggle_relay(relay_state);
    log_event(relay_state);
}

// Toggle the relay state
void toggle_relay(int current_state) {
    int new_state = !current_state;
    gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, new_state);
}

// Log relay state change with timestamp
void log_event(int relay_state) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[80];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fprintf(log_file, "%s - Relay is now %s\n", timestamp, relay_state ? "ON" : "OFF");
        fflush(log_file); // Ensure the data is written to the file immediately
    } else {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Log file is not open.\n");
    }
}

int main() {
    // Set up signal handling for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (init_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1;
    }

    // Open log file
    log_file = fopen("touch_relay.log", "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file!\n");
        gpioTerminate(); // Clean up GPIO before exiting
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up interrupt callback function
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, touch_callback, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error setting up interrupt callback!\n");
        fclose(log_file);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Touch-activated relay control started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop (keep the program running until a signal is received)
    while (running) {
        gpioDelay(100); // Reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Clean up
    gpioSetISRFuncEx(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, NULL, NULL); // Disable interrupt
    fclose(log_file);
    gpioTerminate();

    return 0;
}

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN 25
#define RELAY_PIN 26

// Define log file name
#define LOG_FILE "touch_relay.log"

// Global variables
FILE *log_file = NULL;
int running = 1;

// Function prototypes
int initialize();
void cleanup();
void interrupt_handler(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data);
void log_event(const char *event_description);
void toggle_relay();
void signal_handler(int signal);

int main() {
    // Initialize the system
    if (initialize() != 0) {
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up signal handling for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Main loop (keep the program running until interrupted)
    while (running) {
        // Do nothing, just wait for interrupts
        gpioDelay(100); // Reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Clean up resources before exiting
    cleanup();

    return 0;
}

// Initializes the pigpio library and sets up GPIO pins and interrupts.
int initialize() {
    // Initialize pigpio
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO modes
    if (gpioSetMode(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN to input\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(RELAY_PIN, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set RELAY_PIN to output\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up interrupt on TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, interrupt_handler, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set interrupt for TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Open the log file
    log_file = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        perror("Failed to open log file");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0;
}

// Cleans up resources such as GPIO and pigpio.
void cleanup() {
    // Disable interrupt
    gpioSetISRFuncEx(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, NULL, NULL);

    // Release GPIO resources
    gpioSetMode(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(RELAY_PIN, PI_INPUT);

    // Terminate pigpio
    gpioTerminate();

    // Close the log file
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }
}

// Interrupt handler function that toggles the relay and logs the event.
void interrupt_handler(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data) {
    toggle_relay();
    log_event("Touch detected! Relay toggled.");
}

// Logs an event to the log file with a timestamp.
void log_event(const char *event_description) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    char timestamp[20];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    fprintf(log_file, "%s: %s\n", timestamp, event_description);
    fflush(log_file); // Ensure the log is written immediately
}

// Toggles the state of the relay (HIGH/LOW).
void toggle_relay() {
    int current_level = gpioRead(RELAY_PIN);
    gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, !current_level);
    log_event("Relay toggled.");
}

// Signal handler for Ctrl+C (SIGINT).
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("Ctrl+C pressed, exiting...\n");
    running = 0;
}

#include <stdlib.h>

#include <arpa/inet.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN 25
#define RELAY_PIN 26

// Define server address and port
#define SERVER_IP "192.168.1.100"
#define SERVER_PORT 5000

// Define log file name
#define LOG_FILE "touch_relay.log"

// Global variables
static volatile int running = 1;
static int log_socket = -1;
static FILE *log_file = NULL;

// Function prototypes
void signal_handler(int signal);
void log_event(const char *event);
void touch_interrupt_handler(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick);
int create_socket(const char *server_ip, int server_port);
int connect_socket(int socket_fd, const char *server_ip, int server_port);
int send_data(int socket_fd, const char *data);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    running = 0;
    printf("Caught signal %d, exiting gracefully.\n", signal);
}

// Function to log events to file
void log_event(const char *event) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[20];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    if (log_file == NULL) {
        log_file = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
        if (log_file == NULL) {
            perror("Error opening log file");
            return;
        }
    }

    fprintf(log_file, "%s - %s\n", timestamp, event);
    fflush(log_file);
}

// Interrupt handler for touch sensor
void touch_interrupt_handler(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick) {
    char data[50];
    int relay_state = gpioRead(RELAY_PIN);

    // Toggle relay state
    gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, !relay_state);

    // Log event
    if (level == 1) { // Touch detected
        log_event("Touch detected");
    } else { // Relay changed state
        snprintf(data, sizeof(data), "Relay changed to %d", relay_state);
        log_event(data);
    }

    // Send data to server
    if (send_data(log_socket, data) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error sending data to server.\n");
    }
}

// Function to create a socket
int create_socket(const char *server_ip, int server_port) {
    int socket_fd = socket(AF_INET, SOCK_STREAM, 0);
    if (socket_fd < 0) {
        perror("Error creating socket");
        return -1;
    }
    return socket_fd;
}

// Function to connect to the server
int connect_socket(int socket_fd, const char *server_ip, int server_port) {
    struct sockaddr_in server_addr;
    memset(&server_addr, 0, sizeof(server_addr));
    server_addr.sin_family = AF_INET;
    server_addr.sin_port = htons(server_port);
    if (inet_pton(AF_INET, server_ip, &server_addr.sin_addr) <= 0) {
        perror("Invalid address/ Address not supported");
        return -1;
    }

    if (connect(socket_fd, (struct sockaddr *)&server_addr, sizeof(server_addr)) < 0) {
        perror("Error connecting to server");
        return -1;
    }
    return 0;
}

// Function to send data to the server
int send_data(int socket_fd, const char *data) {
    if (send(socket_fd, data, strlen(data), 0) < 0) {
        perror("Error sending data");
        return -1;
    }
    return 0;
}

int main() {
    // Initialize pigpio
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO modes
    gpioSetMode(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(RELAY_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Set pull-down resistor for touch sensor (optional, depends on sensor)

    // Initialize relay to OFF state
    gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, 0);

    // Set up signal handlers
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    // Create socket
    log_socket = create_socket(SERVER_IP, SERVER_PORT);
    if (log_socket < 0) {
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Connect to server
    if (connect_socket(log_socket, SERVER_IP, SERVER_PORT) < 0) {
        close(log_socket);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up interrupt handler
    if (gpioSetAlertFuncEx(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, touch_interrupt_handler, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error setting up interrupt handler\n");
        close(log_socket);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Touch-activated relay control system running. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (running) {
        sleep(1); // Reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Cleanup
    gpioSetAlertFuncEx(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, NULL, NULL); // Disable interrupt
    close(log_socket);
    gpioTerminate();

    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }

    printf("Exiting.\n");
    return 0;
}

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN 25
#define RELAY_PIN 26

// Debounce delay in milliseconds
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY 50

// Global variables
static volatile int relay_state = 0; // 0 = off, 1 = on
static volatile int running = 1;      // Flag to control the main loop

// Function prototypes
int init_gpio();
void touch_interrupt_handler(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick);
void toggle_relay(int current_state);
void log_event(const char *event_description);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    if (signal == SIGINT) {
        printf("Caught SIGINT, exiting gracefully.\n");
        running = 0; // Set the flag to exit the main loop
        gpioTerminate(); // Terminate the pigpio library
        exit(0);       // Exit the program
    }
}

// Initialize GPIO pins
int init_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    if (gpioSetMode(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as input\n", TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    if (gpioSetMode(RELAY_PIN, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set GPIO %d as output\n", RELAY_PIN);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    if (gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, relay_state) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to write initial state to GPIO %d\n", RELAY_PIN);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Interrupt handler for touch sensor
void touch_interrupt_handler(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick) {
    if (level == 1) { // Rising edge (touch detected)
        toggle_relay(relay_state);
        log_event("Touch detected, relay toggled");
    }
}

// Toggle the relay state
void toggle_relay(int current_state) {
    int new_state = !current_state;
    if (gpioWrite(RELAY_PIN, new_state) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to write to GPIO %d\n", RELAY_PIN);
    }
    relay_state = new_state;
}

// Log event to file with timestamp
void log_event(const char *event_description) {
    FILE *fp = fopen("touch_relay.log", "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        perror("Error opening log file");
        return;
    }

    time_t t = time(NULL);
    struct tm *tm = localtime(&t);
    char timestamp[64];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tm);

    fprintf(fp, "%s - %s\n", timestamp, event_description);
    fclose(fp);
}

int main() {
    // Register signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (init_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1; // Exit if initialization fails
    }

    // Set up interrupt handler for touch sensor
    if (gpioSetAlertFuncEx(TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN, touch_interrupt_handler, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set interrupt handler for GPIO %d\n", TOUCH_SENSOR_PIN);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Touch-activated relay control started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (running) {
        gpioDelay(100); // Sleep for 100ms to reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Cleanup (should not be reached if signal handler works correctly)
    gpioTerminate();
    return 0;
}

    pinMode(pin, OUTPUT);
    digitalWrite(pin, LOW);
    delay(480); // 480us
    pinMode(pin, INPUT);
    delayMicroseconds(70); // 70us
    if (digitalRead(pin) != LOW) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: No DS18B20 sensor found on pin %d\n", pin);
        logEvent("Error: No DS18B20 sensor found");
        return temperature;
    }
    delayMicroseconds(410); // 410us

    // Read scratchpad (9 bytes)
    fd = wiringPiI2CSetup(pin);
    if (fd == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to initialize I2C on pin %d: %s\n", pin, strerror(errno));
        logEvent("Error: Failed to initialize I2C");
        return temperature;
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
        data[i] = wiringPiI2CReadReg8(fd, i);
        if (data[i] == -1) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to read byte %d from pin %d: %s\n", i, pin, strerror(errno));
            logEvent("Error: Failed to read scratchpad byte");
            return temperature;
        }
    }

    // Convert scratchpad to temperature
    int16_t rawTemperature = (data[1] << 8) | data[0];
    temperature = (float)rawTemperature / 16.0;

    return temperature;
}

void controlRGBLED(int red, int green, int blue) {

// Function to log events to a file
void logEvent(const char *message) {
    if (logFile == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Log file is not open.\n");
        return;
    }

    time_t now = time(NULL);
    struct tm *t = localtime(&now);
    fprintf(logFile, "[%04d-%02d-%02d %02d:%02d:%02d] %s\n",
            t->tm_year + 1900, t->tm_mon + 1, t->tm_mday, t->tm_hour, t->tm_min, t->tm_sec, message);
    fflush(logFile); // Ensure the log is written immediately
}

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signalHandler(int signal) {
    keepRunning = 0;
    printf("Caught signal %d, exiting gracefully.\n", signal);
}

int main() {
    // Initialize wiringPi
    if (wiringPiSetup() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to initialize wiringPi: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    // Initialize RGB LED pins as output
    pinMode(RED_PIN, OUTPUT);
    pinMode(GREEN_PIN, OUTPUT);
    pinMode(BLUE_PIN, OUTPUT);

    // Initialize sensor pins
    for (int i = 0; i < numSensors; i++) {
        pinMode(sensorConfigs[i].pin, INPUT);
    }

    // Initialize I2C for each sensor (for reading scratchpad)
    for (int i = 0; i < numSensors; i++) {
        int fd;
        sensorConfigs[i].fd = wiringPiI2CSetup(sensorConfigs[i].pin);
        if (sensorConfigs[i].fd == -1) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to initialize I2C for sensor %d: %s\n", i + 1, strerror(errno));
            logEvent("Error: Failed to initialize I2C for sensor");
        }
    }

    // Open log file
    logFile = fopen("temp_monitor.txt", "a");
    if (logFile == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Failed to open log file: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signalHandler);

    printf("Temperature monitoring started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (keepRunning) {
        bool alertTriggered = false;

        for (int i = 0; i < numSensors; i++) {
            float temperature = readTemperature(sensorConfigs[i].pin);

            if (temperature > -100.0) { // Check for valid temperature reading
                char message[100];
                sprintf(message, "Sensor %d: Temperature = %.2f°C", i + 1, temperature);
                logEvent(message);

                if (temperature > sensorConfigs[i].high_threshold) {
                    alertTriggered = true;
                    sprintf(message, "Alert: Temperature exceeds high threshold for sensor %d (%.2f°C > %.2f°C)", i + 1, temperature, sensorConfigs[i].high_threshold);
                    logEvent(message);
                } else if (temperature < sensorConfigs[i].low_threshold) {
                    alertTriggered = true;
                    sprintf(message, "Alert: Temperature below low threshold for sensor %d (%.2f°C < %.2f°C)", i + 1, temperature, sensorConfigs[i].low_threshold);
                    logEvent(message);
                }
            } else {
                char message[100];
                sprintf(message, "Error reading temperature from sensor %d", i + 1);
                logEvent(message);
            }
        }

        // Trigger visual alert if any sensor exceeds its threshold
        if (alertTriggered) {
            controlRGBLED(255, 0, 0); // Red
            delay(500);
            controlRGBLED(0, 255, 0); // Green
            delay(500);
            controlRGBLED(0, 0, 255); // Blue
            delay(500);
        } else {
            controlRGBLED(0, 0, 0); // Off
        }

        delay(5000); // Check every 5 seconds
    }

    // Cleanup
    controlRGBLED(0, 0, 0); // Turn off LED
    fclose(logFile);

    return 0;
}

void owWriteBit(int bit)
{

void owWriteByte(int byte)
{
    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
    {
        owWriteBit(byte & 1);
        byte >>= 1;
    }
}

int owReadByte()
{
    int byte = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
    {
        byte |= (owReadBit() << (7 - i));
    }
    return byte;
}

void owReset()
{
    owPulse();
    delayMicroseconds(480);
    owPulse();
    delayMicroseconds(70);
}

void owPulse()
{
    digitalWrite(RED_PIN, HIGH);
    delayMicroseconds(1);
    digitalWrite(RED_PIN, LOW);
    delayMicroseconds(60);
}

// Reads the temperature from the DS18B20 sensor
float readTemperature() {
    char device_path[256];
    int fd;
    unsigned char scratchpad[9];
    float temperature = -1000.0; // Initialize with an invalid value

    if (findDS18B20Device(device_path) != 0) {
        logAlert("DS18B20 sensor not found.");
        return temperature;
    }

    fd = wiringPiI2CSetup(0); // Use default I2C address
    if (fd == -1) {
        logAlert("Failed to initialize I2C communication.");
        return temperature;
    }

    if (wiringPiI2CWrite(fd, 0xCC) == -1) {
        logAlert("Failed to reset DS18B20.");
        return temperature;
    }
    delay(10); // Wait for reset

    if (wiringPiI2CWrite(fd, 0x44) == -1) {
        logAlert("Failed to convert T.");
        return temperature;
    }
    delay(750); // Wait for conversion

    if (readScratchpad(fd, scratchpad) == -1) {
        logAlert("Failed to read scratchpad.");
        return temperature;
    }

    int16_t rawTemperature = (scratchpad[1] << 8) | scratchpad[0];
    temperature = (float)rawTemperature / 16.0;

    return temperature;
}

// Reads the scratchpad from the DS18B20 sensor
int readScratchpad(int fd, unsigned char *scratchpad) {

        int data = wiringPiI2CRead(fd);
        if (data == -1) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error reading scratchpad byte %d: %s\n", i, strerror(errno));

        scratchpad[i] = (unsigned char)data;
    }
    return 0;
}

float readTemperature() {

    unsigned char data[9];
    float temperature;
    int crc;

    // Reset the 1-Wire bus
    gpioSetMode(4, PI_OUTPUT); // Use GPIO 4 as output
    gpioWrite(4, 0);
    gpioDelay(480); // 480us
    gpioWrite(4, 1);
    gpioDelay(70); // 70us

    gpioSetMode(4, PI_INPUT); // Set to input
    gpioDelay(10); // Small delay

    // Read the device ID
    unsigned char device_id[8];
    for (i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        device_id[i] = 0;
        int count = 0;
        while (gpioRead(4) == 0 && count++ < 100) {
            gpioDelay(1);
        }
        if (count >= 100) return -1.0; // Error: Bus stuck low

        count = 0;
        while (gpioRead(4) == 1 && count++ < 100) {
            gpioDelay(1);
        }
        if (count >= 100) return -1.0; // Error: Bus stuck high

        if (gpioRead(4) == 0) {
            device_id[i] = (unsigned char)(count * 2);
        } else {
            device_id[i] = (unsigned char)((count * 2) + 1);
        }
    }

    if (strcmp(device_id, DS18B20_DEVICE_ID) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Incorrect device ID: %s\n", device_id);
        return -1.0;
    }

    // Skip ROM command (0xCC)
    for (i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
        gpioWrite(4, 0);
        gpioDelay(60); // 60us
        gpioWrite(4, 1);
        gpioDelay(240); // 240us
    }

    // Send SKIP ROM command
    gpioWrite(4, 0);
    gpioDelay(40); // 40us
    gpioWrite(4, 1);
    gpioDelay(40); // 40us

    // Send CONVERT T command
    gpioWrite(4, 0);
    gpioDelay(40); // 40us
    gpioWrite(4, 1);
    gpioDelay(40); // 40us

    // Wait for conversion (max 75ms)
    gpioDelay(75000); // 75ms

    // Read temperature data
    for (i = 0; i < 9; i++) {
        data[i] = 0;
        int count = 0;
        while (gpioRead(4) == 0 && count++ < 100) {
            gpioDelay(1);
        }
        if (count >= 100) return -1.0; // Error: Bus stuck low

        count = 0;
        while (gpioRead(4) == 1 && count++ < 100) {
            gpioDelay(1);
        }
        if (count >= 100) return -1.0; // Error: Bus stuck high

        if (gpioRead(4) == 0) {
            data[i] = (unsigned char)(count * 2);
        } else {
            data[i] = (unsigned char)((count * 2) + 1);
        }
    }

    crc = 0;
    for (i = 1; i < 8; i++) {

        if (data[i] & 1) {

    int16_t temp_raw = (data[1] << 8) | data[2];
    temperature = (float)temp_raw / 16.0;

    return temperature;
}

#define POSITION_1_FREQ 1500
#define POSITION_2_FREQ 2000
#define POSITION_3_FREQ 2500

void signal_handler(int signal) {

    cleanup_gpio();
    exit(0);
}

    // Register signal handler

    }

        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set ISR function. Exiting.\n");
        cleanup_gpio();
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Rotary encoder and buzzer application started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (1) {

    }

    return 0;
}

// Initializes the pigpio library and sets the GPIO pins as inputs or outputs.
int initialize_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO modes
    gpioSetMode(CLOCK_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(DATA_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(BUZZER_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Set pull-up/down resistors (optional, adjust as needed)
    gpioSetPullUpDown(CLOCK_PIN, PI_PUD_UP);
    gpioSetPullUpDown(DATA_PIN, PI_PUD_UP);

    return 0;
}

// Interrupt service routine for rotary encoder changes.
void encoder_interrupt(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data) {
    static int last_clock_level = 0; // Store the last clock level to detect rising edges
    int data_value;
    static int last_data_value = 0; // Store the last data value to detect changes

    if (level == 1 && last_clock_level == 0) { // Rising edge detected
        data_value = gpioRead(DATA_PIN);

        if (data_value != last_data_value) {
            encoder_position = (encoder_position + 1) % 4; // Increment position
            int frequency = calculate_frequency(encoder_position);

            if (generate_pwm(BUZZER_PIN, frequency) != 0) {

            last_data_value = data_value;
        }
    }

    last_clock_level = level;
}

// Calculates the frequency based on the encoder position.
int calculate_frequency(int position) {
    switch (position) {
        case 0:
            return POSITION_0_FREQ;
        case 1:
            return POSITION_1_FREQ;
        case 2:
            return POSITION_2_FREQ;
        case 3:
            return POSITION_3_FREQ;
        default:
            return 0; // Should not happen, but return 0 in case of error
    }
}

// Generates a PWM signal on the specified pin with the given frequency.
int generate_pwm(int pin, int frequency) {
    if (frequency > 0) {

        gpioSetPWMfrequency(pin, frequency);

    } else {
        gpioPWM(pin, 0); // Turn off buzzer
        return 1; // Failure

}

    gpioTerminate();
