        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set CLOCK_PIN to input\n");
        return -1;
    }
    gpioSetPullUpDown(CLOCK_PIN, PI_PUD_UP); // Enable pull-up resistor

    if (gpioSetMode(DATA_PIN, PI_INPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set DATA_PIN to input\n");
        return -1;
    }
    gpioSetPullUpDown(DATA_PIN, PI_PUD_UP); // Enable pull-up resistor

    if (gpioSetMode(BUZZER_PIN, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set BUZZER_PIN to output\n");
        return -1;
    }

    return 0;
}

// Interrupt handler for encoder position changes
void encoder_interrupt_handler(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data) {
    static int last_level_clock = 0;
    int current_encoder_position = encoder_position;

    if (gpio == CLOCK_PIN) {
        int data_level = gpioRead(DATA_PIN);

        if (level != last_level_clock) {
            if (data_level != gpioRead(DATA_PIN)) {
                current_encoder_position += (level == 1) ? 1 : -1;
            }
        }

        last_encoder_position = current_encoder_position;
        encoder_position = current_encoder_position;
    }
}

// Function to generate a tone on the buzzer
void generate_tone(int frequency) {
    gpioSetPWMfrequency(BUZZER_PIN, frequency); // Set PWM frequency
    gpioPWM(BUZZER_PIN, 128); // 50% duty cycle
}

// Function to cleanup GPIO pins and terminate pigpio
void cleanup_gpio() {
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, 0); // Turn off buzzer
    gpioTerminate();
    printf("pigpio terminated\n");
}

// Signal handler for Ctrl+C
void signal_handler(int signal) {

    printf("Ctrl+C pressed, exiting...\n");

#include <stdlib.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define CLK_PIN 23
#define DT_PIN 24
#define BUZZER_PIN 20

// Global variables
volatile int encoder_position = 0;
volatile int frequency = 0;

// Function prototypes
int initialize_gpio();
void encoder_isr(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data);
void update_frequency();
void cleanup_gpio();
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, cleaning up...\n", signal);
    cleanup_gpio();
    exit(0);
}

// Initialize GPIO pins
int initialize_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    // Set GPIO modes
    if (gpioSetMode(CLK_PIN, PI_INPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set CLK_PIN mode\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(DT_PIN, PI_INPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set DT_PIN mode\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetMode(BUZZER_PIN, PI_OUTPUT) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set BUZZER_PIN mode\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Register interrupt service routine for encoder
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(DT_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, encoder_isr, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set ISR for DT_PIN\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Interrupt service routine for encoder
void encoder_isr(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data) {
    static int last_level = 0; // Store the last level to detect rising edges
    if (level == 1 && last_level == 0) { // Only trigger on rising edge
        encoder_position++;
        update_frequency();
    }
    last_level = level;
}

// Update frequency based on encoder position
void update_frequency() {
    // Map encoder position (0-255) to frequency (200Hz - 2000Hz)
    frequency = 200 + (encoder_position * 1800) / 255;

    // Clamp frequency to valid range
    if (frequency < 200) {
        frequency = 200;
    } else if (frequency > 2000) {
        frequency = 2000;
    }

    // Set PWM frequency
    if (gpioSetPWMfrequency(BUZZER_PIN, frequency) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set PWM frequency\n");
    }
}

// Cleanup GPIO resources
void cleanup_gpio() {
    gpioTerminate();
}

int main() {
    // Register signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (initialize_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1;
    }

    // Main loop (keep running until a signal is received)
    while (1) {
        // Do nothing, just let the ISR and frequency update handle things
        gpioDelay(100); // Sleep for 100ms to reduce CPU usage
    }

    return 0;
}

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define CLOCK_PIN 23
#define DATA_PIN 24
#define BUZZER_PIN 20

// Define PWM frequency (Hz)
#define PWM_FREQUENCY 1000

// Define buzzer duration (ms)
#define BUZZER_DURATION 200

// Debounce time (us)
#define DEBOUNCE_TIME 5000

// Global variables
static volatile int encoder_position = 0;
static volatile unsigned long last_interrupt_time_clk = 0;
static volatile unsigned long last_interrupt_time_dt = 0;
static volatile int running = 1; // Flag for signal handler

// Function prototypes
int initialize_gpio();
void encoder_interrupt_clk(int gpio, int level, unsigned int tick, void *user_data);
void encoder_interrupt_dt(int gpio, int level, unsigned int tick, void *user_data);
void set_buzzer_frequency(int position);
void play_buzzer(int duration_ms);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Function to initialize GPIO pins
int initialize_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO modes
    gpioSetMode(CLOCK_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(DATA_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(BUZZER_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Set PWM frequency
    if (gpioSetPWMfrequency(BUZZER_PIN, PWM_FREQUENCY) != PWM_FREQUENCY) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set PWM frequency\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Register interrupt handlers
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(CLOCK_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, encoder_interrupt_clk, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set ISR for CLOCK_PIN\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(DATA_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, encoder_interrupt_dt, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set ISR for DATA_PIN\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0;
}

// Interrupt handler for CLK pin
void encoder_interrupt_clk(int gpio, int level, unsigned int tick, void *user_data) {
    unsigned long current_time = tick;
    if (current_time - last_interrupt_time_clk > DEBOUNCE_TIME) {
        // Read DATA pin state
        int data_pin_state = gpioRead(DATA_PIN);
        encoder_position += data_pin_state;
        set_buzzer_frequency(encoder_position);
        play_buzzer(BUZZER_DURATION);
        last_interrupt_time_clk = current_time;
    }
}

// Interrupt handler for DT pin
void encoder_interrupt_dt(int gpio, int level, unsigned int tick, void *user_data) {
    unsigned long current_time = tick;
    if (current_time - last_interrupt_time_dt > DEBOUNCE_TIME) {
        // Read CLOCK pin state
        int clock_pin_state = gpioRead(CLOCK_PIN);
        if (clock_pin_state == 1) {
            // Increment encoder position only if DT is high
            encoder_position++;
        } else {
            // Decrement encoder position only if DT is high
            encoder_position--;
        }
        set_buzzer_frequency(encoder_position);
        play_buzzer(BUZZER_DURATION);
        last_interrupt_time_dt = current_time;
    }
}

// Function to set buzzer frequency based on encoder position
void set_buzzer_frequency(int position) {
    // Map encoder position (0-360) to frequency (200Hz - 2000Hz)
    int frequency = 200 + (position * 1800 / 360); // Linear mapping
    if (frequency < 200) frequency = 200;
    if (frequency > 2000) frequency = 2000;

    // Set PWM duty cycle (50%)
    gpioPWM(BUZZER_PIN, 128); // 50% duty cycle (128/255)
    gpioSetPWMfrequency(BUZZER_PIN, frequency);
}

// Function to play buzzer for a specified duration
void play_buzzer(int duration_ms) {
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, 1); // Turn buzzer on
    gpioDelay(duration_ms * 1000); // Delay for duration_ms milliseconds
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, 0); // Turn buzzer off
}

// Signal handler for Ctrl+C
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("Ctrl+C pressed. Exiting gracefully...\n");
    running = 0; // Set flag to exit loop
    gpioTerminate(); // Terminate pigpio
    exit(0);
}

int main() {
    // Register signal handler
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (initialize_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Rotary encoder and buzzer program started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop (keep running until Ctrl+C is pressed)
    while (running) {
        // Do other tasks here (e.g., process encoder data, etc.)
        gpioDelay(100); // Sleep for 100ms to reduce CPU usage
    }

    return 0;
}

#include <pigpio.h>

#include <signal.h>

// Define GPIO pins

#define BUZZER_PIN 16

// Define calibration values
#define LOW_THRESHOLD 1000  // Adjust based on sensor readings in no flame conditions
#define HIGH_THRESHOLD 500  // Adjust based on sensor readings in flame conditions

// Define log file name
#define LOG_FILE "fire_alerts.log"

// Global variables
static volatile int running = 1; // Flag to control the main loop
static FILE *log_file = NULL;

// Function prototypes
int initialize_gpio();
int read_flame_sensor();
void activate_buzzer();
void log_event();
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Function to initialize GPIO pins
int initialize_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    gpioSetMode(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(BUZZER_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Function to read the flame sensor
int read_flame_sensor() {
    int reading = gpioRead(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN);
    return reading;
}

// Function to activate the buzzer
void activate_buzzer() {
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, 1); // Turn buzzer on
    gpioDelay(1000);          // Wait for 1 second
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, 0); // Turn buzzer off
}

// Function to log the event to a file
void log_event() {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[80];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fprintf(log_file, "%s - Flame detected!\n", timestamp);
        fflush(log_file); // Ensure the data is written to the file immediately
    } else {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Log file is not open.\n");
    }
}

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("Caught signal %d, exiting gracefully...\n", signal);
    running = 0; // Set the flag to exit the main loop
}

int main() {
    // Register signal handler for SIGINT (Ctrl+C)
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (initialize_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1; // Exit if initialization fails
    }

    // Open log file
    log_file = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file!\n");
        gpioTerminate(); // Clean up GPIO before exiting
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Flame sensor monitoring started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (running) {
        int sensor_value = read_flame_sensor();

        // Calibration and error recovery
        if (sensor_value > HIGH_THRESHOLD) {
            sensor_value = 0; // Reset to a reasonable value
        } else if (sensor_value < LOW_THRESHOLD) {
            sensor_value = 1; // Reset to a reasonable value
        }

        if (sensor_value == 0) { // Flame detected (LOW signal)

            activate_buzzer();
            log_event();

        gpioDelay(100); // Delay to avoid excessive CPU usage
    }

    // Cleanup
    printf("Cleaning up...\n");
    fclose(log_file);
    gpioTerminate();

    return 0;
}

#include <string.h>
#include <time.h>

#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define FLAME_SENSOR_PIN 27
#define BUZZER_PIN 16

// Define constants
#define CALIBRATION_TIME 5 // Seconds
#define LOG_FILE "fire_alerts.log"

// Global variables (to be minimized, but needed for signal handling)
static volatile int running = 1;
static FILE *log_file = NULL;

// Function prototypes
int initialize();
int read_flame_sensor();
void activate_buzzer(int duration_ms);
void log_event(const char *message);
void signal_handler(int signal);
int calibrate_sensor();

int main() {
    if (initialize() != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Initialization failed. Exiting.\n");
        return 1;
    }

    if (calibrate_sensor() != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Sensor calibration failed. Exiting.\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Flame sensor monitoring started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (running) {
        if (read_flame_sensor() == 0) { // Flame detected (LOW signal)
            activate_buzzer(500); // Activate buzzer for 500ms
            log_event("Flame detected!");
        }
        gpioDelay(100); // Check every 100ms
    }

    gpioTerminate();
    printf("Program terminated.\n");
    return 0;
}

int initialize() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    gpioSetMode(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(BUZZER_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Open log file
    log_file = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file!\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Register signal handler for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    return 0;
}

int read_flame_sensor() {
    int value = gpioRead(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN);
    if (value < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error reading flame sensor value!\n");
        log_event("Error reading sensor value");
        return 1; // Indicate error
    }
    return value;
}

void activate_buzzer(int duration_ms) {
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, 1); // Turn buzzer on
    gpioDelay(duration_ms);
    gpioWrite(BUZZER_PIN, 0); // Turn buzzer off
}

void log_event(const char *message) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[20];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    fprintf(log_file, "%s: %s\n", timestamp, message);
    fflush(log_file); // Ensure immediate write to file
    printf("%s: %s\n", timestamp, message);
}

void signal_handler(int signal) {
    running = 0;
    printf("Ctrl+C pressed. Exiting...\n");
}

int calibrate_sensor() {
    printf("Calibrating sensor...\n");
    int sum = 0;
    int count = 0;
    time_t start_time = time(NULL);

    while (time(NULL) - start_time < CALIBRATION_TIME) {
        int value = read_flame_sensor();
        if (value >= 0) { // Valid reading
            sum += value;
            count++;
        }
        gpioDelay(50); // Read every 50ms
    }

    if (count > 0) {
        int average = sum / count;
        printf("Calibration complete. Average sensor value: %d\n", average);
        return 0; // Calibration successful
    } else {
        fprintf(stderr, "Calibration failed. No valid readings.\n");
        return 1; // Calibration failed
    }
}

[/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <wiringPi.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <unistd.h>
#include <errno.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define FLAME_SENSOR_PIN 27
#define BUZZER_PIN 16

// Define log file name
#define LOG_FILE "fire_alerts.log"

// Debounce delay in milliseconds
#define DEBOUNCE_DELAY 50

// Buzzer activation duration in milliseconds
#define BUZZER_DURATION 200

// Sensor calibration offset (adjust this value)
#define CALIBRATION_OFFSET 50 // Example: 50 milliseconds

// Global variables
static volatile int running = 1;
static FILE *log_file = NULL;

// Function prototypes
int init_gpio(void);
int read_flame_sensor(void);
void activate_buzzer(int duration_ms);
void log_event(void);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Structure for configuration (optional)
typedef struct {
    int flame_pin;
    int buzzer_pin;
    int log_file_name;
    int debounce_delay;
    int buzzer_duration;
} Config;

// Function to initialize GPIO pins
int init_gpio(void) {
    if (wiringPiSetupGpio() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error initializing wiringPi: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return -1;
    }

    pinMode(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN, INPUT);
    pinMode(BUZZER_PIN, OUTPUT);

    return 0;
}

// Function to read the flame sensor with debouncing
int read_flame_sensor(void) {
    int reading = digitalRead(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN);
    delay(DEBOUNCE_DELAY);
    return reading;
}

// Function to activate the buzzer
void activate_buzzer(int duration_ms) {
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, HIGH);
    delay(duration_ms);
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);
}

// Function to log the event to file
void log_event(void) {
    time_t now = time(NULL);
    struct tm *tstruct = localtime(&now);
    char buf[80];
    strftime(buf, sizeof(buf), "%Y-%m-%d.%X", tstruct);

    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fprintf(log_file, "%s - Flame detected!\n", buf);
        fflush(log_file); // Ensure the data is written to disk immediately
    } else {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error: Log file is not open.\n");
    }
}

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("Caught signal %d, exiting gracefully.\n", signal);
    running = 0;
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW); // Ensure buzzer is off
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }
    exit(0);
}

int main(void) {
    Config config = {
        .flame_pin = FLAME_SENSOR_PIN,
        .buzzer_pin = BUZZER_PIN,
        .log_file_name = LOG_FILE,
        .debounce_delay = DEBOUNCE_DELAY,
        .buzzer_duration = BUZZER_DURATION
    };

    // Set up signal handling for Ctrl+C
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (init_gpio() == -1) {
        return 1;
    }

    // Open log file
    log_file = fopen(config.log_file_name, "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Flame sensor monitoring started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    while (running) {
        int sensor_value = read_flame_sensor();
        if (sensor_value == LOW) { // Flame detected (LOW signal)
            printf("Flame detected!\n");
            log_event();
            activate_buzzer(config.buzzer_duration);
            delay(config.debounce_delay); // Debounce after activation
        } else {
            delay(10); // Small delay to reduce CPU usage
        }
    }

    // Clean up and exit (should not reach here if signal handler works)
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }
    return 0;
}

#include <signal.h>
#include <errno.h>
#include <wiringPi.h>

// Define GPIO pins (wiringPi numbering)
#define FLAME_SENSOR_PIN 27
#define BUZZER_PIN 16

// Define log file path
#define LOG_FILE "fire_alerts.log"

// Debounce time in milliseconds
#define DEBOUNCE_TIME 50

// Buzzer activation time in milliseconds
#define BUZZER_DURATION 500

// Global variables
static volatile bool flame_detected = false;
static volatile unsigned long last_trigger_time = 0;
static FILE *log_file = NULL;

// Function prototypes
int init_gpio(void);
int init_logging(void);
void cleanup_gpio(void);
void cleanup_logging(void);
void signal_handler(int signal);
void flame_alert_callback(void);

// Function to initialize GPIO pins
int init_gpio(void) {
    if (wiringPiSetupGpio() == -1) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error initializing wiringPi: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return -1;
    }

    pinMode(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN, INPUT);
    pinMode(BUZZER_PIN, OUTPUT);

    return 0;
}

// Function to initialize logging
int init_logging(void) {
    log_file = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file: %s\n", strerror(errno));
        return -1;
    }

    return 0;
}

// Function to clean up GPIO pins
void cleanup_gpio(void) {
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);
}

// Function to clean up logging
void cleanup_logging(void) {
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }
}

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    printf("Caught signal %d, cleaning up...\n", signal);
    cleanup_gpio();
    cleanup_logging();
    exit(0);
}

// Callback function for flame detection
void flame_alert_callback(void) {
    unsigned long current_time = millis();

    // Debounce
    if (current_time - last_trigger_time < DEBOUNCE_TIME) {
        return;
    }

    last_trigger_time = current_time;

    // Activate buzzer
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, HIGH);
    delay(BUZZER_DURATION);
    digitalWrite(BUZZER_PIN, LOW);

    // Get current timestamp
    time_t now = time(NULL);
    struct tm *tstruct = localtime(&now);
    char timestamp[80];
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", tstruct);

    // Log event
    fprintf(log_file, "%s - Flame detected!\n", timestamp);
    fflush(log_file);

    printf("%s - Flame detected!\n", timestamp);
    flame_detected = true; // Set the global flag
}

int main(void) {
    // Install signal handler
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize GPIO
    if (init_gpio() == -1) {
        return 1;
    }

    // Initialize logging
    if (init_logging() == -1) {
        cleanup_gpio();
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Flame sensor monitoring started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (1) {
        if (flame_detected) {
            // Register callback function for debouncing
            if (wiringPiISR(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN, INT_EDGE_FALLING, flame_alert_callback) < 0) {
                fprintf(stderr, "Unable to setup ISR: %s\n", strerror(errno));
                break;
            }
        } else {
            // Unregister the ISR when no flame is detected
            wiringPiISR(FLAME_SENSOR_PIN, INT_EDGE_FALLING, NULL);
        }

        delay(10); // Small delay to reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Clean up and exit
    cleanup_gpio();
    cleanup_logging();

    return 0;
}

#include <signal.h>

#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define VIBRATION_PIN 15
#define RED_LED_PIN 5
#define GREEN_LED_PIN 6

// Vibration intensity levels
#define INTENSITY_LOW 1
#define INTENSITY_HIGH 2

// File to log vibration events
#define LOG_FILE "vibration_log.txt"

// Global variables
static volatile int running = 1;
static FILE *log_fp = NULL;

// Function prototypes
int initialize();
void vibration_detected(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data);
int log_vibration(int intensity);
void activate_led(int intensity);
void cleanup();
void signal_handler(int signal);

int main() {
    // Install signal handler for graceful shutdown
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);

    // Initialize the system
    if (initialize() != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Initialization failed. Exiting.\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Main loop (keep the program running until a signal is received)
    while (running) {
        gpioDelay(100); // Reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Clean up and exit
    cleanup();

    return 0;

// Initializes the pigpio library and sets up the GPIO pins.
int initialize() {
    // Initialize pigpio
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO modes
    gpioSetMode(VIBRATION_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(RED_LED_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);
    gpioSetMode(GREEN_LED_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Set pull-up/down resistor (optional, adjust as needed)
    gpioSetPullUpDown(VIBRATION_PIN, PI_PUD_DOWN); // Example: pull-down

    // Open the log file
    log_fp = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (log_fp == NULL) {
        perror("Error opening log file");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up interrupt handler
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(VIBRATION_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, vibration_detected, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set up interrupt handler\n");
        fclose(log_fp);
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    return 0;
}

// Interrupt handler function.
void vibration_detected(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data) {
    int intensity = gpioRead(VIBRATION_PIN); // Read the intensity level
    log_vibration(intensity);
    activate_led(intensity);
}

// Logs the vibration event with a timestamp and intensity level to the log file.
int log_vibration(int intensity) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[20];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    if (log_fp != NULL) {
        fprintf(log_fp, "%s - Vibration intensity: %d\n", timestamp, intensity);
        fflush(log_fp); // Ensure data is written to the file immediately
        return 0; // Success
    } else {
        fprintf(stderr, "Log file not open\n");
        return 1; // Failure
    }
}

// Activates the two-color LED warning system based on the intensity level.
void activate_led(int intensity) {
    if (intensity == INTENSITY_HIGH) {
        gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 1); // Turn on red LED
        gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 0); // Turn off green LED
    } else if (intensity == INTENSITY_LOW) {
        gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 0); // Turn off red LED
        gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 1); // Turn on green LED
    } else {
        gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 0); // Turn off both LEDs
    }
}

// Cleans up resources before exiting.
void cleanup() {
    printf("Cleaning up...\n");

    // Disable interrupt handler
    gpioSetISRFuncEx(VIBRATION_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, NULL, NULL);

    // Turn off LEDs
    gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 0);
    gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 0);

    // Close log file
    if (log_fp != NULL) {
        fclose(log_fp);
    }

    // Terminate pigpio
    gpioTerminate();
}

// Signal handler for SIGINT (Ctrl+C).
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    running = 0;
    printf("Received signal %d. Exiting gracefully.\n", signal);
}

#include <signal.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN 15
#define LED_RED_PIN 5
#define LED_GREEN_PIN 6

// Define vibration intensity levels
#define INTENSITY_LOW 0
#define INTENSITY_MEDIUM 1
#define INTENSITY_HIGH 2

// Define the log file name
#define LOG_FILE "vibration_log.txt"

// Global variables for file pointer and program state
FILE *log_file = NULL;
volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

// Function prototypes
void vibration_detected(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data);
int log_vibration_event(int intensity);
void activate_led(int intensity);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Interrupt handler function
void vibration_detected(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data) {
    if (gpio == VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN && level == 1) { // Rising edge detected
        // Determine the vibration intensity based on the duration the signal is high
        int intensity = 0;
        uint32_t start_time = tick;
        while (gpioRead(VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN) == 1 && program_running) {
            gpioDelay(1000); // Check every 1ms
            uint32_t current_time = tick;
            if (current_time - start_time >= 50000) { // 50ms threshold
                intensity = INTENSITY_HIGH;
                break;
            } else if (current_time - start_time >= 25000) { // 25ms threshold
                intensity = INTENSITY_MEDIUM;
                break;
            } else if (current_time - start_time >= 12500) { // 12.5ms threshold
                intensity = INTENSITY_LOW;
                break;
            }
        }

        // Log the vibration event
        if (log_vibration_event(intensity) != 0) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error logging vibration event.\n");
        }

        // Activate the LED based on the intensity
        activate_led(intensity);
    }
}

// Function to log vibration events to a file
int log_vibration_event(int intensity) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[20];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    if (log_file == NULL) {
        log_file = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
        if (log_file == NULL) {
            fprintf(stderr, "Error opening log file: %s\n", LOG_FILE);
            return -1;
        }
    }

    if (fprintf(log_file, "%s, Intensity: %d\n", timestamp, intensity) < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error writing to log file.\n");
        return -1;
    }

    fflush(log_file); // Ensure data is written to the file immediately

    return 0;
}

// Function to activate the LED based on the vibration intensity
void activate_led(int intensity) {
    switch (intensity) {
        case INTENSITY_HIGH:
            gpioWrite(LED_RED_PIN, 1);
            gpioWrite(LED_GREEN_PIN, 0);
            break;
        case INTENSITY_MEDIUM:
            gpioWrite(LED_RED_PIN, 0);
            gpioWrite(LED_GREEN_PIN, 1);
            break;
        case INTENSITY_LOW:
            gpioWrite(LED_RED_PIN, 0);
            gpioWrite(LED_GREEN_PIN, 0);
            break;
        default:
            gpioWrite(LED_RED_PIN, 0);
            gpioWrite(LED_GREEN_PIN, 0);
            break;
    }
}

// Signal handler function to gracefully terminate the program
void signal_handler(int signal) {
    program_running = 0;
    printf("Caught signal %d, exiting gracefully...\n", signal);
}

int main() {
    // Initialize pigpio library
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO pins as input or output
    gpioSetMode(VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(LED_RED_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);
    gpioSetMode(LED_GREEN_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Register the interrupt handler function
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, vibration_detected, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error setting up interrupt handler.\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up signal handlers for graceful termination
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    printf("Vibration monitoring system started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop (keep the program running until a signal is received)
    while (program_running) {
        sleep(1); // Reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Clean up and exit
    gpioTerminate();
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }

    printf("Vibration monitoring system stopped.\n");

    return 0;
}

void vibration_callback(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick) {
    int intensity = NO_VIBRATION;

    if (level == 0) { // Falling edge

    } else if (level == 1) { // Rising edge

    } else { // Stable state

            gpioWrite(LED_RED_PIN, 1);

<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to detect vibrations using a SW-420 vibration sensor on GPIO 15, implementing interrupt-driven detection to log shock events with timestamps and intensity levels to 'vibration_log.txt', activating a two-color LED warning system on GPIO pins 5 and 6, with comprehensive error handling for interrupt setup and file operations. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <string.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <pigpio.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN 15
#define LED1_PIN 5
#define LED2_PIN 6

// Define intensity levels
#define INTENSITY_LOW 1
#define INTENSITY_HIGH 2

// Define log file name
#define LOG_FILE "vibration_log.txt"

// Function prototypes
void vibration_callback(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data);
int log_event(const char *timestamp, const char *intensity);
void control_leds(int intensity);
void signal_handler(int signal);

// Global variables
static volatile int running = 1;

// Signal handler function
void signal_handler(int signal)
{
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, cleaning up and exiting...\n", signal);
    running = 0;
    gpioTerminate();
    exit(0);
}

// Interrupt handler function
void vibration_callback(int gpio, int level, uint32_t tick, void *user_data)
{
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[80];
    const char *intensity;

    // Get current time
    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);
    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    // Determine intensity based on level
    if (level == 1) {
        intensity = "LOW";
    } else {
        intensity = "HIGH";
    }

    // Log the event
    if (log_event(timestamp, intensity) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error logging event.\n");
    }

    // Control the LEDs
    control_leds(level);
}

// Function to log events to file
int log_event(const char *timestamp, const char *intensity)
{
    FILE *fp = fopen(LOG_FILE, "a");
    if (fp == NULL) {
        perror("Error opening log file");
        return -1;
    }

    fprintf(fp, "%s, %s\n", timestamp, intensity);
    fclose(fp);
    return 0;
}

// Function to control LEDs
void control_leds(int intensity)
{
    if (intensity == INTENSITY_HIGH) {
        gpioWrite(LED1_PIN, 1);
        gpioWrite(LED2_PIN, 0);
    } else {
        gpioWrite(LED1_PIN, 0);
        gpioWrite(LED2_PIN, 1);
    }
}

int main()
{
    // Initialize pigpio
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1;
    }

    // Set GPIO pins as input/output
    gpioSetMode(VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(LED1_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);
    gpioSetMode(LED2_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    // Set pull-up/down resistor (optional, adjust as needed)
    gpioSetPullUpDown(VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN, PI_PUD_UP);

    // Initialize LEDs to off state
    gpioWrite(LED1_PIN, 0);
    gpioWrite(LED2_PIN, 0);

    // Set up interrupt handler
    if (gpioSetISRFuncEx(VIBRATION_SENSOR_PIN, RISING_EDGE, 0, vibration_callback, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Error setting up interrupt handler\n");
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up signal handlers for graceful shutdown
    signal(SIGINT, signal_handler);
    signal(SIGTERM, signal_handler);

    printf("Vibration monitoring started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (running) {
        gpioDelay(100); // Sleep for 100ms to reduce CPU usage
    }

    // Clean up and exit
    gpioTerminate();
    return 0;
}

/*
To install pigpio:
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install pigpio

To start the pigpio daemon:
sudo pigpiod
*/

To compile:

To run:
./vibration_monitor

<s><s>[INST]  Write a C program for Raspberry Pi 4 Model B using `pigpio` library to detect vibrations using a SW-420 vibration sensor on GPIO 15, implementing interrupt-driven detection to log shock events with timestamps and intensity levels to 'vibration_log.txt', activating a two-color LED warning system on GPIO pins 5 and 6, with comprehensive error handling for interrupt setup and file operations. [/INST]  #include <stdio.h>
#include <stdlib.h>
#include <pigpio.h>
#include <time.h>
#include <signal.h>
#include <string.h>

// Define GPIO pins
#define VIBRATION_PIN 15
#define RED_LED_PIN 5
#define GREEN_LED_PIN 6

// Define vibration intensity levels
#define LOW_INTENSITY 1
#define MEDIUM_INTENSITY 2
#define HIGH_INTENSITY 3

// Global variables
FILE *log_file = NULL;
volatile sig_atomic_t program_running = 1;

// Function prototypes
int setup_gpio();
int setup_interrupt();
void log_vibration(int intensity);
void control_leds(int intensity);
void cleanup(int signal);

// Signal handler for graceful shutdown
void cleanup(int signal) {
    program_running = 0;
    printf("\nCaught signal %d, cleaning up...\n", signal);

    // Disable interrupt
    gpioSetAlertFuncEx(VIBRATION_PIN, NULL, NULL);

    // Turn off LEDs
    gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 0);
    gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 0);

    // Close log file
    if (log_file != NULL) {
        fclose(log_file);
    }

    // Terminate pigpio
    gpioTerminate();

    exit(0);
}

// Function to set up GPIO pins
int setup_gpio() {
    if (gpioInitialise() < 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "pigpio initialisation failed\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    gpioSetMode(VIBRATION_PIN, PI_INPUT);
    gpioSetMode(RED_LED_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);
    gpioSetMode(GREEN_LED_PIN, PI_OUTPUT);

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Function to set up interrupt handling
int setup_interrupt() {
    if (gpioSetAlertFuncEx(VIBRATION_PIN, NULL, NULL) != 0) {
        fprintf(stderr, "Failed to set alert function for vibration pin\n");
        return 1; // Indicate failure
    }

    return 0; // Indicate success
}

// Function to log vibration event to file
void log_vibration(int intensity) {
    time_t rawtime;
    struct tm *timeinfo;
    char timestamp[80];

    time(&rawtime);
    timeinfo = localtime(&rawtime);

    strftime(timestamp, sizeof(timestamp), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S", timeinfo);

    log_file = fopen("vibration_log.txt", "a");
    if (log_file == NULL) {
        perror("Error opening log file");
        return;
    }

    fprintf(log_file, "%s, Intensity: %d\n", timestamp, intensity);
    fclose(log_file);
}

// Function to control LEDs based on vibration intensity
void control_leds(int intensity) {
    if (intensity == LOW_INTENSITY) {
        gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 0);
        gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 1);
    } else if (intensity == MEDIUM_INTENSITY) {
        gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 1);
        gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 1);
    } else if (intensity == HIGH_INTENSITY) {
        gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 1);
        gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 0);
    } else {
        gpioWrite(RED_LED_PIN, 0);
        gpioWrite(GREEN_LED_PIN, 0);
    }
}

int main() {
    // Register signal handlers for Ctrl+C and termination signals
    signal(SIGINT, cleanup);
    signal(SIGTERM, cleanup);

    // Set up GPIO
    if (setup_gpio() != 0) {
        return 1;
    }

    // Set up interrupt
    if (setup_interrupt() != 0) {
        gpioTerminate();
        return 1;
    }

    printf("Vibration monitoring started. Press Ctrl+C to exit.\n");

    // Main loop
    while (program_running) {
        // Vibration detected (interrupt handler)
        gpioDelay(100); // Debounce
    }

    return 0;
}
